Title: Over-expression of mutated ZmDA1 or ZmDAR1 gene improves maize kernel yield by enhancing starch synthesis

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: DH4866 (transgenic recipient); B73 used as cDNA source for cloning
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Dominant-negative overexpression of the R358K-mutated ZmDA1 allele (Zmda1) is a promising strategy to raise kernel yield and starch content in maize. Best deployed with endosperm/yield trait selection and possibly stacked with sugar-import or starch-biosynthetic capacity genes, but caution is needed because overexpression of the wild-type ZmDA1 reduced kernel weight and yield, indicating dosage/allele specificity is critical.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of mutated ZmDA1 increased kernel number, kernel weight, endosperm weight, starch content, soluble sugar during early kernel development, BETL development, and overall grain yield; overexpression of wild-type ZmDA1 had the opposite effect.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression of mutated and wild-type alleles in maize; 3-year field trials; qRT-PCR; kernel histology/paraffin and semi-thin sections; Schiff staining; starch and soluble sugar quantification; SEM of starch granules; yeast two-hybrid for interaction with ZmSWEET4c.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field trials in Jinan, China (2013-2015); developing kernels sampled across DAP3-DAP25; interpretation depends on early grain-filling/endosperm development stage.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ubiquitously expressed; highest in leaves and immature tassels/ears; lower than ZmDAR1 in post-pollination kernels/endosperm.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ubiquitously expressed in maize; highest in leaves and immature tassels/ears.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ZmDA1 is a DA1-family ubiquitin receptor/peptidase homolog of Arabidopsis AtDA1. The engineered allele carries the dominant-negative R358K substitution analogous to Arabidopsis da1-1. The paper infers ZmDA1 may regulate sugar transport and starch synthesis indirectly, possibly by affecting stability/cleavage of partners or substrates rather than acting as a transcription factor or metabolic enzyme.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Wild-type ZmDA1 overexpression reduced kernel weight and yield. Mutated allele caused only slight vegetative changes, but precise allele choice and expression level are important to avoid negative yield effects.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DA1 family ubiquitin receptor/peptidase; single LIM domain, two UIM domains, DUF3633
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: B73 (cloning source); functional tests in DH4866 background
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmDA1 is a maize DA1-family ubiquitin receptor homolog, and the R358K-mutated form (Zmda1) acts as a dominant-negative allele that improves maize yield. 2. Zmda1 overexpression increased ear length, kernel number, kernel weight, endosperm proportion, starch accumulation, and early soluble sugar levels. 3. Zmda1 enhanced BETL development and upregulated ZmMRP1, ZmSWEET4c, ZmGBSSI, and ZmBt2, linking this gene to improved sugar import and starch synthesis.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major carbon storage reserve in grain; determinant of kernel weight and grain yield.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: DH4866 (transgenic recipient); B73 used as cDNA source for cloning
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Dominant-negative overexpression of the R322K-mutated ZmDAR1 allele (Zmdar1; generated by a single-base G to A mutation at bp 966) is a strong candidate for maize yield improvement, especially for endosperm development and starch accumulation. It may be valuable in yield-focused engineering, but wild-type overexpression should be avoided because it decreases kernel weight and yield.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of mutated ZmDAR1 increased kernel number, kernel weight, endosperm weight, starch content, soluble sugar, BETL development, and grain yield; wild-type ZmDAR1 overexpression reduced kernel weight and yield.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression of mutated and wild-type alleles in maize; 3-year field trials; qRT-PCR; kernel anatomical analyses; starch and soluble sugar assays; SEM; Southern blot/PCR confirmation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field trials in Jinan, China (2013-2015); developing kernels assessed at DAP3-DAP25; strong phenotype during endosperm development and grain filling.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ubiquitously expressed; primarily expressed in kernels at DAP10-DAP15, with low expression in embryos at DAP15.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ubiquitously expressed in maize; strongest in kernels at DAP10-DAP15.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Wild-type ZmDAR1 overexpression reduced kernel weight and yield. Precise use of the mutated allele rather than native overexpression is essential.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DA1 family ubiquitin receptor/peptidase; single LIM domain, two UIM domains, DUF3633
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: B73 (cloning source); functional tests in DH4866 background
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmDAR1 is a maize DA1-related ubiquitin receptor homolog with strong expression in developing kernels. 2. The mutated allele Zmdar1 increased grain yield across field trials by increasing kernel number, kernel size, endosperm weight, soluble sugar, and starch content. 3. Zmdar1 promoted BETL development and upregulated genes associated with transfer-cell differentiation and starch synthesis, supporting a causal role in sugar import and endosperm starch deposition.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major carbon storage reserve in grain; determinant of kernel weight and grain yield.
